pages.raw_text:
BOISE, Nov. 27.-The examina-
tion of talesmen IN the trial of
George A. Pettibone, charged with
completely In the murder of Ex-
Gov. 'Stuenenberg, was begun in
the district court here at 10 O'Clock
this morning. James H. Hawley
chief counsel for the state. ar
rived on time hut Darrow was not
yet present.


c. c. Cavenaugh. partner of Sen
Borah, was entered as associate
counsel for the state. barrow was
reported on the way from Spokane


Early in the examination of tales.
men 1t became evident that diff.
Guilty will be experienced in obtain.
ing a jury, The first four exam.
ined, r. f. Lamb, G. W. Mitchell
F. M. Blair, ]. S. ID. Manville, all
farmers, were excused because of
objection to capital punishment or
opinion formed at hearings of the
Haywood trial.


The state passed w. H. Gebberd
for cause. He is a farmer.


The state challenged Henry Con
rad for congratulating Haywood on
the verdict in that trial. Alex Hof
feger was accepted by the state
He is a French civil war veteran
who never reads the papers. Jesse
Sailor was challenged by the state
for bias. Frank Gardner was per
emptorially challenged by the de.
